narrative_arc: ordinary redhead walks the city → triggers a secret transformation into muscle-hero Ultragal → flies off and smashes attacking robots → meets a rival heroine (Domina) → they brawl → Domina reveals her own deception and explosively muscle-transforms to match → escalating size-vs-size duel → Ultragal appears to win, but the twist reveals a lab-coated mastermind controlling the fight, leaving Ultragal manipulated (to be continued)

panels.notes: Hero-shot cover: Ultragal flying toward camera, bicep flexed up, cape billowing, low angle from the city below makes her tower. Title logo ULTRAGAL + Domina's Deception. Credits 'Art by Boogie / Story by Gribble', GrowGetterComics.com, 'Extra special thanks to Alpha Centaurian'.

pages.page_notes: distance_spread=1; flat_panel_count=0; single full-bleed cover, strong low-hero framing establishes the muscle-power promise immediately.

panels.notes: Establishing street shot: redhead 'Mira' in yellow top + floral skirt walking a city block, pedestrians and buildings receding in perspective. Empty speech bubble. Strong fg/bg depth down the avenue.
Profile close-up of Mira with glasses, mild pensive look. Speed-line background. Sets the mild-mannered alter ego.
Down-angle on her yellow flats stepping toward a dark alley/grate — she ducks out of sight to transform. Narrow alley gives vertical depth.
Tight close-up: Mira grins knowingly, hand tugging her collar — the 'about to change' beat. First readable expression; raked composition.

pages.page_notes: distance_spread=3; flat_panel_count=0; clean four-panel setup that hooks fast by page-end — establishes ordinary girl + her secret with good distance variety and a final intent grin.

panels.notes: Transformation trigger: Mira arches back, hair lifting, head thrown back mid-gasp against a hot pink/orange power field. Body leaning on a strong diagonal. Energy bars rising at frame bottom.
Money-shot ECU: bust swelling and straining against the yellow tube top, fabric beginning to give. Radiating burst lines. First clothing-destruction beat.
ECU on torso/abs hardening and expanding, skirt waistband stretching. Burst-line energy. Progressive zoom on a second body region.
ECU on glutes/hips ballooning, floral skirt riding up and tearing. Third progressive body-part zoom in the stacked right column.
Tightest ECU, lower-body mass filling frame as growth peaks for this page. Stacked-column device runs chest→abs→glutes→glutes top-to-bottom.

pages.page_notes: distance_spread=2; flat_panel_count=0; the Ultragal transformation engine — left full-figure ecstasy panel paired with a right-side stacked column of progressive body-part ECUs and RIIIP/KRRRK clothing-tear SFX. Textbook multi-panel-progressive + clothing-destruction + sfx-driven.

panels.notes: Peak chest/pec ECU exploding outward, deep burst lines, big onomatopoeia. Maximum-mass money shot.
Shoulder/upper-arm ECU swelling, FWOOMP cape/aura beat. Continues the peak across body regions.
Arms/biceps ECU at full size, 'FLEX' tag. Peak muscle mass, fists clenching. Bottom-left of the page.

Payoff reveal: Ultragal's new face, eyes closed in a satisfied grin, red hair, gold clasp + white costume now visible. Aura WZZZ sparks. Lands the 'new self' emotionally — best face on the transformation.

pages.page_notes: distance_spread=2; flat_panel_count=0; peak page — three explosive body-part ECUs (chest/shoulders/arms) with BABAAAM escalation, capped by a satisfied face reveal. Heavy zoom coverage, but the three growth ECUs themselves are faceless (intensity 0) — sensation is carried entirely by SFX + burst lines, not expression.

panels.notes: Full-figure splash of completed Ultragal launching upward between skyscrapers, one fist flexed, cape fluttering. Worm's-eye low angle, buildings converging for depth. 'HELL YEAH!' sells her delight. Full-body-reveal of the transformed result.

pages.page_notes: distance_spread=1; flat_panel_count=0; single splash, the full-body-reveal payoff of the Ultragal transformation. Strong low-hero + converging-building depth; best 'showing off the new body' beat in the issue.

panels.notes: Ultragal rockets across frame, fist thrust toward camera, giant SWOOOOSH lettering. Strong motion diagonal + foreshortened punch-arm.
Aerial extreme-wide: tiny Ultragal flying over a sprawling city grid far below. Excellent scale-contrast / establishing breather between action beats.
Diving figure on a hard diagonal, ZOOM SFX, hair and cape trailing. Dynamic motion staging.
Superhero landing in a crouch, radial impact burst, FWAAM. Power-pose touchdown into the next scene.

pages.page_notes: distance_spread=3; flat_panel_count=0; a flight/transit beat that is camera-dynamic throughout — SWOOOOSH push-in, an EWS aerial for scale, a ZOOM dive, a FWAAM landing. Good example of keeping a non-growth travel page kinetic.

panels.notes: Splash: three hulking white combat robots loom in the background while the dark-haired rival heroine 'Domina' (green/blue costume, blue cape) stands confident mid-frame; Ultragal's huge cape-and-glutes form fills the foreground from behind. Layered scale-contrast (big FG hero, mid villain, tall robots). Introduces Domina and the robot threat.

pages.page_notes: distance_spread=1; flat_panel_count=0; villain-and-threat reveal splash with strong three-layer depth and scale-contrast. Best use of foreground-mass + midground-figure staging in the book.

panels.notes: Ultragal arms-crossed, dwarfed between two towering robot torsos closing in. Low-hero but threat-framed; sets the fight.
Giant robot fist swings down, foreshortened toward camera, big WHAAAM. Impact framing.
Ultragal counter-kicks a robot on a diagonal, KRUUNK. Dynamic action axis.
She braces and shoves a robot arm, biceps engaged. Mid exchange.
Robot smashed apart, debris, CRAASSHH. Mid-page beat.
A robot hand/head sparks and dies, KRZAARK. Closes the robot-smash with sound.

pages.page_notes: distance_spread=3; flat_panel_count=0; six-panel robot-fight with loud SFX (WHAAAM/KRUUNK/CRAASSHH/KRZAARK) and varied angles. Action reads clearly; faces during impacts default a bit flat (panels 2 & 6 are inanimate-focused).

panels.notes: Inset: a wrecked robot head rolls past Ultragal's boot. Aftermath detail leading into the confrontation.
Ultragal and Domina face off nose-to-nose, profile two-shot, tension between them. The rivalry ignites.
Hard close-up of Ultragal's glaring face, brow furrowed — strongest anger read so far. Sells the heel-turn into the heroine-vs-heroine duel.
The two grapple/clash, costumes tangling on a diagonal. First blow between them.
Domina hurled back / thrown, dynamic toss. Mid-fight.
Big impact, Ultragal lunging, KRAGOOSSH lettering bottom-center. Punchy fight beat with a snarling face.

pages.page_notes: distance_spread=3; flat_panel_count=0; the heroine-vs-heroine fight kicks off — good escalating two-shot → CU rage → grapple progression. Faces carry this page better than the robot page (two intensity-4 rage CUs).

panels.notes: Ultragal taunting profile, gold clasp prominent. Trash-talk beat.
Domina poised confidently in front of a damaged robot, gesturing — she's hinting at her plan. KRRZT machine spark.
Domina full-figure, hand on hip, blue cape spread — confident villain posture before the turn.
Over-Ultragal's-shoulder onto Domina, red hair sweeping foreground. OTS depth.
Domina takes a hit, doubled over, WHUMP starburst. Fight continues.

pages.page_notes: distance_spread=2; flat_panel_count=0; rivalry/standoff page — lots of smug posturing and an OTS. Distance band tightens to MS/MCU (less spread than the action pages); carried by character attitude rather than scale variety.

panels.notes: Ultragal grabs Domina by the costume, both snarling, raked confrontation axis. Hostility peaks.
Ultragal lands a blow, Domina's head snaps, WHAM lettering. Strong action + furious faces.
Down-angle on Domina knocked to the ground, Ultragal standing over. High-vuln makes Domina small — she's losing (the deception's bait).
Domina crumpled, looking up. Sets up the reversal — she's about to spring her transformation.
Sudden energy surge between them, XZAAAPP, Domina's eyes flashing — the deception triggers. Cliff into the Domina growth scene.

pages.page_notes: distance_spread=2; flat_panel_count=0; Ultragal dominates the fight (high-vuln on Domina twice) right before the reversal — good dramatic-irony staging. XZAAAPP on the last panel is the turn into Domina's transformation.

panels.notes: Ultragal crouched over a fallen robot/Domina, low-hero glutes-forward pose, a clawed robot hand reaching in foreground. Tension before the surge.
Scramble/grapple, costumes and limbs tangling on a bright color-field. Mid-beat.
Domina shoves back with crackling energy (WZZZ) along her arm — power beginning to rise. Foreshortened thrust.
Energy arcs between them, both straining. Building to the transformation.
Pull-back wide: the two sprawled across rubble, Domina down but crackling. Resets scale before the growth page.

pages.page_notes: distance_spread=3; flat_panel_count=0; transitional brawl that seeds Domina's WZZZ power buildup across three panels. Good spread (MCU→WS) and energy-SFX foreshadowing of the transformation.

panels.notes: Domina rises with arms spreading, grinning wide, chest swelling, blue costume tightening; KYZZZ electric arcs. The deception transformation begins — strong open-mouthed glee.
Small inset of Ultragal: wide-eyed, hand to mouth, recoiling — the reaction-intercut. Best 'oh no' beat; sells that Domina is now the threat.
Domina's face, eyes wild, mouth open in manic delight as power floods in. Peak-intensity villain ecstasy.
Bust and shoulders expanding, arcs crackling off her body. Growth visibly progressing.
Tighter on the gleeful face, fangs of a grin, electricity framing. Maintains peak expression intensity.
Bottom wide-ish panel: noticeably bulkier Domina settling into a confident grin, costume stretched. End of the early growth stage.

pages.page_notes: distance_spread=3; flat_panel_count=0; Domina's transformation OPENS strong on expression — unlike Ultragal's faceless TF, this one is face-forward (two intensity-5 ecstasy CUs) plus the key Ultragal reaction-intercut inset. Reaction-intercut + sfx-driven + early growth-focus.

panels.notes: Domina mid-power-up, fists clenched at her sides, lightning lashing all around, screaming with delight. Maximum strain/ecstasy; whole-body growth.
Extreme manic close-up, eyes whited-out, teeth bared — terrifying power-high. Peak villain face of the issue.
Arm/shoulder muscle swelling, costume splitting at the seams, CRACKLE. Clothing-destruction begins in earnest.
Pec/chest ECU bursting toward camera against an orange burst-field, BAAM. Money-shot mid-growth.

pages.page_notes: distance_spread=3; flat_panel_count=0; the mid stage of Domina's growth — pairs two peak-intensity faces (ecstasy/rage) with a foreshortened muscle ECU. This page proves the studio CAN put a high-intensity face on a growth beat (contrast with p4's faceless peak).

panels.notes: Domina lunging, green top straining and tearing across the bust, RIIIP. Clothing-destruction in motion with a snarling face.
Costume splits open across the chest, fabric peeling, wicked grin. Garment failure advances.
ECU of bust at full size, last shreds of the green top giving way. Peak clothing-destruction money-shot.
Lower body swelling, leggings/shorts splitting down the thighs, RIIIP. Growth spreads to legs.
ECU thighs/hips at max mass, last of the costume tearing. Caps the clothing-destruction sequence before the full reveal.

pages.page_notes: distance_spread=2; flat_panel_count=0; the clothing-destruction climax of Domina's TF — five RIIIP beats walking the garment failure chest→legs. Heavy growth-focus, but like p3-4 the body ECUs go faceless (intensity 0); the two upper panels keep a snarl/smirk.

panels.notes: Full-figure splash of the fully-transformed Domina, both arms in a double-biceps flex, head back roaring with joy, towering over a cracked/devastated cityscape with her old shoes blown off. Worm's-eye low-hero, FLEX tags. The full-body-reveal payoff of her transformation — and the only growth SPLASH that pairs peak muscle with a peak (intensity-5) face.

pages.page_notes: distance_spread=1; flat_panel_count=0; Domina's full-body-reveal splash — the strongest single growth page in the issue because it lands maximum mass AND a maximum face simultaneously. Mirror of p5 (Ultragal's reveal) but bigger and angrier.

panels.notes: Hulked-out Domina flexing and gloating, dwarfing the frame. Now the size-dominant rival.
Ultragal pointing, alarmed/shouting — reacting to Domina's new size. Reaction beat with a strong face.
Ultragal charges in on a diagonal, ZOOOOM. Re-engaging the now-bigger foe.
Domina braces, ground-shaking THOOOM. Power on her side now.
Bodies collide, WHAAAM starburst, Domina's bulk clearly overpowering. The size-vs-size duel proper begins.

pages.page_notes: distance_spread=3; flat_panel_count=0; round two with the size table turned — ZOOOOM/THOOOM/WHAAAM escalation and consistently expressive faces. Scale-contrast now does story work (Domina bigger).

panels.notes: Domina backhands Ultragal across frame, TWHAACK. Hard horizontal action line.
Domina laughing as she lands a body blow, WHUUMP. She's winning and enjoying it.
Both flexing/locked up, Domina's mass against Ultragal's, CLENCH. Power-struggle staging.
Domina swings Ultragal overhead/down, double SFX, Ultragal grimacing. Ultragal on the ropes.

pages.page_notes: distance_spread=1; flat_panel_count=0; brutal exchange — Domina dominating with TWHAACK/WHUUMP/WHAAAMM. Distance is monotone (all MS) which slightly flattens the page, but the diagonals, scale-contrast and grimaces keep impact energy up.

panels.notes: Domina's massive torso shoved chest-first toward a smaller Ultragal, sneering down. Size-contrast intimidation.
Ultragal hand-to-chest, stunned at the power gap. Underdog reaction beat.
Ultragal squares up again despite the disadvantage, Domina looming. Rallying.
Ultragal drives a chest-to-chest counter, WHOOOOPP. She fights back into the clash.

pages.page_notes: distance_spread=2; flat_panel_count=0; the muscle-vs-muscle power struggle, heavy on scale-contrast and OTS to sell Domina's size edge. Ends on Ultragal's WHOOOOPP comeback push.

panels.notes: Chest-to-chest mutual shove, impact starburst between them, CRUUSH. Peak of the power struggle, both straining grins.
Grapple/lock, Domina's bulk bearing down, Ultragal gritting. Mid-struggle.
Big hit lands, BLAM lettering across the bottom. Climactic blow of the duel.
Ultragal pressing in, energy crackling — momentum swinging her way before the resolution. CRACKLE.

pages.page_notes: distance_spread=2; flat_panel_count=0; the climactic CRUUSH/BLAM muscle-clash. Strong scale-contrast + foreshortening; faces consistently strained/determined (no dead faces in the climax).

panels.notes: Ultragal flies up triumphant over the city, fist forward (apparent victor of the brawl), high aerial with buildings below. WHOOM. The surface 'win'.
Down-angle on the DEFEATED dark-haired Domina face-down in the dirt/rubble beside a wrecked control-robot, dazed. High-vuln. The 'Domina' Ultragal fought was a decoy/puppet.
Reveal: a lab-coated woman (the real mastermind, dark hair) holding a handheld control device, watching coolly. The DECEPTION lands — the whole fight was engineered/controlled.
Gloved hand reaches down toward the beaten Domina's face — she's being collected/controlled. Helplessness beat.
Final panel: the mastermind raises a finger and smirks in triumph — she's in control despite Ultragal's apparent win. Pull-forward cliffhanger button on the villain's smug face.

pages.page_notes: distance_spread=3; flat_panel_count=0; the twist/denouement-as-cliffhanger — intercuts Ultragal's hollow aerial 'victory' with the lab-coated puppeteer revealing the deception. Good ironic juxtaposition; ends on the villain in control (to be continued).

panels.notes: Credits page over a faded ghost of the Ultragal cover figure. 'Art by Boogie / Written by Gribble / Produced by GrowGetterComics.com / Colors by Little L. & Shockoi / Special thanks to SuperCDR, Masu_Lavu, Ichiban' + a two-column Patreon supporter list. Not a story panel.

pages.page_notes: distance_spread=1; flat_panel_count=0; back-matter credits/Patreon page, not narrative. Confirms full creator credits.

strengths: Two full transformation set-pieces, not one: Ultragal (p3-4) and Domina (p13-16). The Domina TF is the better-built of the two — it leads with peak-intensity faces (p13 panel3 & p14 panel2 are intensity-5 ecstasy/rage) AND cuts to Ultragal's hand-to-mouth shock inset (p13 panel2), so the growth carries emotion instead of relying only on SFX.

Disciplined multi-panel-progressive + clothing-destruction engines: p3 runs a stacked right-column of chest→abs→glutes ECUs with RIIIP/KRRRK; p15 walks Domina's garment failure chest→legs across five RIIIP beats. This is exactly the progressive body-part zoom device the pipeline wants more of.

Mirror-structured full-body reveals: p5 (Ultragal) and p16 (Domina) are worm's-eye low-hero splashes that pay off each transformation; p16 is the single best growth page because it lands max muscle AND a max (intensity-5) face at once.

Consistently dynamic camera: across 22 pages essentially zero flat-level panels. Heavy use of low-hero, foreshortened punches/limbs, diagonals, OTS, and scale-contrast; p7's three-layer depth (big FG hero / mid villain / tall robots) is a standout composition.

SFX do real sensation work: BABAAAM/BAAAM for growth bursts, RIIIP/KRRRK for tearing cloth, KYZZZ for the electric power-up, and a full WHAAAM/KRUUNK/CRAASSHH fight vocabulary — lettering carries beats with almost no dialogue.

A genuine narrative twist: the title 'deception' pays off on p21 — Ultragal's aerial 'win' is intercut with a lab-coated mastermind controlling the whole fight, ending on a smug pull-forward cliffhanger rather than a flat KO.
weaknesses: Ultragal's own transformation (p3-4) goes faceless at the peak: every growth ECU on p4 (chest/shoulders/arms) is intensity-0, so her marquee moment is sold entirely by SFX and burst-lines. Compare Domina's TF, which keeps a screaming face in frame — the studio knows how, but didn't apply it to the hero.

Body-detail ECUs default to blank faces as a pattern (p3 all four ECUs, p4 panels1-3, p15 panels3-5 all intensity 0). When the body fills the frame the face vanishes and with it the emotional register; a few inset reaction cuts would fix it cheaply.

Setup is almost nonexistent and exposition is opaque: only one true setup page (p2) before the trigger, and with all dialogue balloons left EMPTY the reader can't tell who Domina is, why she's fighting, or what the lab-coat reveal means without guessing. Great for pacing, weak for stakes/clarity.

Distance variety sags in the mid-brawl: p18 is four straight MS panels (distance_spread=1) and p10 tightens to MS/MCU posturing — the robot/flight pages (p6, p8) are far more cinematically varied than the heroine-vs-heroine exchanges.

Round-1 vs round-2 fights are structurally repetitive: p9-12 and p17-20 are both 'rivals trade blows' sequences with similar framing; the Domina TF in between is what separates them, so the second brawl risks feeling like more-of-the-same padding before the twist.
